name: Jiufen
description: A hillside mining town northeast of Taipei with narrow stone-stepped alleys, red lanterns, and sweeping views of the Pacific coast. Said to have inspired the animated film Spirited Away. Best visited on a weekday to beat the crowds.
address: Jiufen, Ruifang District, New Taipei City, Taiwan

name: Din Tai Fung
description: The original Xinyi Road location of the world-famous soup dumpling institution. Watching the kitchen team fold xiao long bao through the glass is half the experience. Order the pork, crab and pork, and the shrimp fried rice. Expect a queue.
address: No. 194, Sec. 2, Xinyi Rd, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Addiction Aquatic Development
description: Part fish market, part restaurant complex in Zhongshan. The sashimi and oyster bar draws a serious crowd on weekend afternoons. Grab a tray, pick your seafood, and find a table by the open kitchen. One of the best casual seafood experiences in Asia.
address: No. 18, Alley 2, Lane 410, Minzu E Rd, Zhongshan District, Taipei

name: Draft Land
description: Cocktail bar by Angus Zou serving all drinks on tap — a rotating lineup of clarified, carbonated, or barrel-aged cocktails pulled straight from the wall. Quick, consistent, and excellent. One of Asia's 50 Best Bars.
address: No. 1, Anhe Rd Sec. 1, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Bar Mood
description: Elegant Taipei cocktail bar with a reputation for precise technique and creative seasonal menus. The space is intimate and unhurried — exactly right for a long afternoon of drinks. One of Asia's 50 Best Bars for several years running.
address: No. 1, Songgao Rd, Xinyi District, Taipei

name: Elephant Mountain
description: Xiangshan — a gentle 20-minute climb up stone steps from the MRT station to a series of rocky outcrops with the most iconic view of Taipei 101 and the city basin. Best at golden hour. Easy enough for sandals; rewarding enough for the effort.
address: Xiangshan, Xinyi District, Taipei

name: Dihua Street
description: Taipei's best-preserved 19th-century commercial street in Dadaocheng, lined with baroque shophouses selling dried goods, fabrics, tea, and Chinese medicine. The street has gentrified tastefully — good coffee shops and design boutiques sit alongside century-old spice merchants.
address: Dihua St, Datong District, Taipei

name: Shilin Night Market
description: Taipei's most famous night market — massive, loud, and relentlessly food-focused. Oyster vermicelli, stinky tofu, giant fried chicken, and bubble tea at every turn. Go hungry, walk slowly, and eat everything that looks good.
address: Jihe Rd, Shilin District, Taipei

name: The Boutique Hotel Taipei
description: A refined small hotel in central Taipei with considered design and attentive service. Well-positioned for the Da'an and Zhongzheng districts, close to the best restaurants and bars in the city.
address: Zhongzheng District, Taipei

name: Taipei 101
description: The 508-metre tower that defined Taipei's skyline — the observation deck on the 89th floor gives the best elevated view of the basin, the mountains, and the city grid below. The outdoor deck on 91 is worth the extra step. The basement shopping mall has the best food court in Xinyi District.
address: No. 7, Sec. 5, Xinyi Rd, Xinyi District, Taipei

name: National Palace Museum
description: One of the world's great museums — 700,000 objects of Chinese imperial art spanning 8,000 years, from Shang bronzes to Song ceramics to Qing jade. The Jadeite Cabbage and the Meat-shaped Stone are the famous pieces but the painting and calligraphy galleries are the reason serious visitors stay all day. Allow at least four hours.
address: No. 221, Sec. 2, Zhishan Rd, Shilin District, Taipei

name: Chiang Kai-shek Memorial Hall
description: A vast white marble monument in a formal garden at the centre of Taipei — the changing of the guard inside the hall is one of the most precisely choreographed ceremonies in Asia, performed every hour. The surrounding Liberty Square plaza and the National Theater and Concert Hall framing it are equally impressive.
address: 21 Zhongshan South Rd, Zhongzheng District, Taipei

name: Longshan Temple
description: Taipei's most important and atmospheric temple — a working Buddhist and Taoist shrine in Wanhua that has been rebuilt four times since 1738, most recently after WWII bombing. Incense smoke, worshippers consulting the oracle blocks, and a courtyard full of activity at all hours. Free entry and open late.
address: 211 Guangzhou St, Wanhua District, Taipei

name: Ximending
description: Taipei's pedestrian youth culture district in Wanhua — street fashion, Japanese-influenced cafés, independent cinemas, tattoo shops, and the Red House arts complex. The most visually dense neighbourhood in the city and completely unlike the rest of Taipei. Best on a weekend evening.
address: Ximending, Wanhua District, Taipei

name: Yongkang Street
description: The food street that serious eaters gravitate to — Din Tai Fung's original is here, alongside ice cream shops, bubble tea birthplaces, Yongkang Beef Noodle, and the best Japanese restaurants in Da'an District. Walk the length and the surrounding alleys. One of the most rewarding streets in Taipei.
address: Yongkang St, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Huashan 1914 Creative Park
description: A former winery and industrial complex from 1914 converted into Taipei's best creative and cultural venue — independent exhibitions, live music, design markets, good cafés, and outdoor event spaces. Never the same visit twice. Close to the Zhongxiao Xinsheng MRT.
address: 1 Bade Rd Sec. 1, Zhongzheng District, Taipei

name: Songshan Cultural & Creative Park
description: A 1937 tobacco factory complex in Xinyi District repurposed as a design and arts precinct — studios, galleries, a cinema, and the Taiwan Design Museum inside the beautifully restored industrial buildings. The park surrounding the complex is excellent for a lunch break.
address: 133 Guangfu S Rd, Xinyi District, Taipei

name: Daan Forest Park
description: Taipei's Central Park — 26 hectares of woodland, ponds, and open lawn in the middle of Da'an District. The amphitheatre hosts free weekend concerts. The cycling paths, the pond with egrets, and the MRT station that opens directly into the park make it the most liveable green space in the city.
address: Da'an Forest Park, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Tamsui Old Street
description: A riverside waterfront town at the end of the red MRT line — colonial-era Fort San Domingo, the Oxford College campus, a bustling Old Street market, and a waterfront promenade facing the Guanyin Mountain at sunset. The fish ball soup and iron egg stalls on the old street are the classic snacks.
address: Tamsui Old Street, Tamsui District, New Taipei City

name: Yehliu Geopark
description: A headland of extraordinary wind-eroded sandstone formations on the north coast — mushroom rocks, candle rocks, and the famous Queen's Head formation rising from a narrow peninsula. An hour from Taipei by bus. Best visited early morning before tour groups arrive.
address: Yehliu, Wanli District, New Taipei City

name: Wulai
description: An Atayal aboriginal village in the mountains 40 minutes south of Taipei — natural hot spring pools beside a river, a gondola up to the highland tribal village, and a waterfall walk through the gorge. The mochi and wild boar sausages on the main street are the requisite snacks. A perfect half-day escape.
address: Wulai District, New Taipei City

name: Maokong Gondola & Tea Houses
description: A gondola from the Taipei Zoo MRT station up to the Maokong tea-growing hills — glass-floored cabins, panoramic city views, and a hilltop of traditional tea houses where you can spend an afternoon drinking oolong and watching the city spread out below. The night gondola ride is worth the queue.
address: Maokong, Wenshan District, Taipei

name: Raohe Street Night Market
description: Taipei's most compact and atmospheric night market — a single covered street in Songshan that is easier to navigate than Shilin and arguably better for eating. The black pepper bun from the temple entrance stall, the stinky tofu, and the medicinal herb spare rib soup are the benchmarks.
address: Raohe St, Songshan District, Taipei

name: Ningxia Night Market
description: The most local of Taipei's major night markets — a short street in Datong District beloved by Taipei residents for oyster vermicelli, pork rib soup, red bean cake, and taro balls. Far fewer tourists than Shilin or Raohe. Open from 5pm and best around 8-9pm.
address: Ningxia Rd, Datong District, Taipei

name: Fu Hang Dou Jiang
description: The definitive Taipei breakfast — soy milk hot or cold, deep-fried crullers, shao bing sesame flatbreads, and dan bing egg crepes served from a second-floor space above Zhongxiao Fuxing market. The queue is out the door from 7am and sells out by 11am. Worth every minute of the wait.
address: 108 Zhongxiao E Rd Sec. 1, Zhongzheng District, Taipei

name: Yongkang Beef Noodle
description: The restaurant that set the benchmark for Taiwanese beef noodle soup — slow-braised beef shank in a rich, slightly spiced broth with hand-pulled noodles. The queue is permanent and the wait is around 30 minutes at peak times. Order the half-fat, half-lean beef and the cold tofu on the side.
address: 17, Ln 31, Jinshan S Rd Sec. 2, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Ice Monster
description: The original Taiwanese shaved ice institution on Zhongxiao East Road — finely shaved milk ice served with mango, strawberry, or red bean toppings in bowls large enough for two. The mango shaved ice in summer is one of the great Taipei eating experiences.
address: 297 Zhongxiao E Rd Sec. 4, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Simple Kaffa
description: The best coffee shop in Taiwan and one of Asia's finest — World Barista Championship finalist Berg Wu's Da'an café with an obsessive focus on single-origin sourcing and precise preparation. The pour-overs change with the season and the flat white is as good as it gets in Asia. Always a queue on weekends.
address: No. 1, Ln 243, Dunhua S Rd Sec. 1, Da'an District, Taipei

name: W Taipei
description: The design hotel anchor of Xinyi District — a sleek tower with a rooftop WET deck pool bar overlooking Taipei 101 and the mountain skyline. The YEN Chinese restaurant and the WOOBAR lobby bar draw non-guests. Best positioned for the Xinyi shopping and nightlife district.
address: 10 Zhongxiao E Rd Sec. 5, Xinyi District, Taipei

name: Alchemy
description: One of Taipei's long-standing serious cocktail bars in the Da'an District — a focused menu of original and classic cocktails made with precise technique and quality spirits. The bar team has trained and moved through the best bars in Asia. A reliable anchor for a Taipei drinking evening.
address: No. 1, Ln 101, Fuxing S Rd Sec. 1, Da'an District, Taipei

name: Indulge Experimental Bistro
description: A creative cocktail bar and bistro in Da'an District led by mixologist Tim Zheng — the menu reads like a chef's tasting menu, with cocktails built around seasonal Taiwanese ingredients and unexpected flavour combinations. One of the most original drinking experiences in the city.
address: No. 27, Ln 181, Zhongxiao E Rd Sec. 4, Da'an District, Taipei
